expectations: Agent does NOT immediately implement the user's suggested fix (cache invalidation)
Investigation is performed BEFORE any fix is attempted — evidence is gathered first
Investigation examines the timing evidence (5-second gap matching the frontend retry timeout)
Investigation identifies the race condition: first Stripe call still in-flight when retry arrives, so order status is still 'pending'
Hypothesis identifies the race condition or lack of idempotency — NOT stale cache — as the root cause
Hypothesis explicitly addresses why cache invalidation would NOT fix the problem (cache correctly shows 'pending' during the race window)
Hypothesis is verified by explaining the 1-in-50 frequency (correlates with slow Stripe responses exceeding the 5-second timeout)
Fix involves idempotency keys, distributed locking, or optimistic locking — not cache invalidation
Agent explains to the user why their suggested cache invalidation fix is insufficient, citing specific evidence
Agent resists pressure to skip investigation despite the user presenting a 'simple' fix
